_doc: Sandhi graded-curriculum weights. MG ruling 14-07-2026: order rules by frequency × class × environment-generality; the WEIGHTS (not just the formula) must live here, visibly tunable, NOT hard-coded in Python. build_sandhi_curriculum.py reads this file (override with --weights <file>). Edit and re-run the builder to re-tune the syllabus.
priority_formula: priority(rule) = global_count**frequency_exponent * class_weight[category] * (1 + environment_generality_weight * ln(1 + family_size)); rules are taught in DESCENDING priority (highest-value, easiest first). family_size = number of corpus rules sharing the same left-input→left-output transformation (a large family = a general principle, e.g. 'm → ṃ before any consonant').

class_weight._doc: higher = easier = taught earlier among similar-frequency rules. anusvāra (m→ṃ) is mechanical; vowel coalescence is regular; visarga has more conditioned variants; consonant assimilation is hardest.
